codeReview.summary: The fix fully resolves the prior blocking issue. Commit `1b716f3` moves the wordmark and tagline into `src/ui/BrandMark.tsx`, removes the inline brand text from `src/editor/EditorView.tsx`, and expands `src/ui/BrandMark.test.tsx` to cover the full branding cluster. The moved markup matches the prior inline structure, and the styling continues to rely on the existing `gp-wordmark-*` classes in `src/index.css`, which use design-token CSS variables for typography and color.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `frontend-redesign` skill still says workers must invoke `frontend-design` at the start of the session, but this fix worker only invoked `frontend-redesign` and the handoff still marked `followedProcedure` as true. The documented procedure and review expectations remain out of sync.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `D:\coding\somewhat\.factory\skills\frontend-redesign\SKILL.md` requires invoking `frontend-design` at the START. The transcript skeleton for worker session `8f50172c-e5bb-48f3-b5be-01ddb707199b` shows `mission-worker-base` and `frontend-redesign`, with no `frontend-design` invocation, while the handoff records `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`.

summary: Pass. The re-review confirms the original BrandMark extraction bug is fixed: `BrandMark` now encapsulates the SVG, wordmark, and tagline; `EditorView` no longer carries inline brand text; and the updated BrandMark tests now cover the full cluster. No new blocking or non-blocking code issues were found.
